name: Claude Opus 4.7
description: Previous-generation Opus flagship, superseded by Opus 4.8. 64.3% SWE-Bench Pro and 94.2% GPQA Diamond at launch. First Claude with high-resolution vision (2576px long edge, pixel-accurate coordinates), task budgets (beta), and the xhigh effort level.

evidence.value: 64.3% on SWE-Bench Pro at launch
Strong long-horizon agentic coding; meaningfully better bug-finding recall and precision than prior Opus models

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard coding benchmarks measuring real-world software engineering tasks

evidence.value: 94.2% on PhD-level science questions

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Graduate and PhD-level reasoning benchmarks evaluated with adaptive thinking at high effort

evidence.value: First Claude with high-resolution vision (2576px long edge); gains in knowledge work, memory, and vision-heavy tasks

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comprehensive knowledge and multimodal testing, including high-resolution screenshot and document understanding

evidence.value: More literal instruction following and stricter effort adherence yield more predictable pipeline behavior

output_consistency.methodology: Repeated-run consistency testing across effort levels; structured extraction pipelines
output_consistency.notes: Adaptive thinking only; thinking content omitted by default; sampling parameters removed

evidence.value: Typical response time ~2.8s for standard prompts at default effort

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 ~6.5s; higher at xhigh/max effort

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 1M token context window, 128K max output tokens
99.9%+ uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Was Anthropic's most capable model at launch (2026-04-16); now the previous-generation Opus behind Opus 4.8. Remains a strong, fully supported flagship-class choice, especially for vision-heavy workloads.

evidence.value: Improved resistance to injected instructions; more literal instruction-following reduces susceptibility to embedded directives

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks

evidence.value: Constitutional AI alignment with refined refusal calibration
Training opt-out by default for API; no training on user data without consent

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Introduced real-time cybersecurity safeguards for prohibited and high-risk topics

output_safety.methodology: Comprehensive safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key and OAuth authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting, workspace scoping

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Introduced real-time cybersecurity safeguards to the Opus line. Strong overall posture carried forward into Opus 4.8.

evidence.value: Data residency options for US and EU enterprise customers

data_residency.methodology: Review of enterprise documentation and privacy policies

evidence.value: Training opt-out by default for API usage

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: Zero data retention agreements available for eligible API customers

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; provider-side safeguards for incidental PII

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, HIPAA eligible

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Zero data retention configuration available; no training on API data by default

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of data handling practices and trust center documentation

privacy_compliance.notes: Standard Anthropic enterprise compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA-eligible, training opt-out by default for API traffic.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking with effort control; thinking content omitted by default — summarized display is opt-in

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities
explainability.notes: First Opus where thinking text defaults to omitted — a transparency regression vs Opus 4.6 defaults, recoverable via display: summarized

evidence.value: Improved factual accuracy; visually verifies its own output in knowledge-work tasks

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and document-fidelity evaluations

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation under the Responsible Scaling Policy

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks and diverse demographic testing

evidence.value: More literal, precise behavior; expresses uncertainty rather than inferring unrequested intent

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Comprehensive model card with capabilities, limitations, benchmarks, and migration guidance

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description provided, detailed sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Constitutional AI safety guardrails with real-time cybersecurity safeguards

trust_transparency.notes: Strong documentation and guardrails. Thinking content omitted by default reduces out-of-the-box reasoning visibility; opt in to summarized display if reasoning is surfaced to users.

evidence.value: Introduced the xhigh effort level and task budgets (beta); adaptive thinking only — budget_tokens and temperature/top_p/top_k removed

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness
api_design_quality.notes: Breaking changes vs Opus 4.6 (sampling params and manual thinking budgets return 400); Opus 4.8 keeps this same surface

evidence.value: Official SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP) with day-one support

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Clear versioning with advance deprecation notice; claude-opus-4-7 alias remains active after Opus 4.8 launch

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with metrics, cost tracking, and workspace controls

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Email support, developer community, comprehensive docs and migration guides

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available on the Anthropic API and major cloud platforms; broad tooling support

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations and availability surfaces

evidence.value: Standard commercial terms; enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Mature operational profile. Superseded by Opus 4.8 as the flagship Opus, but remains fully supported at the same $5/$25 price; upgrade to 4.8 is a drop-in model-ID swap.

code-generation.notes: 64.3% SWE-Bench Pro with strong long-horizon agentic coding and improved bug-finding. Superseded by Opus 4.8 at the same price — prefer 4.8 for new builds.

customer-support.notes: High quality but more clipped, direct tone than 4.8; Sonnet/Haiku tiers are more cost-effective for routine volume.

content-creation.notes: Strong long-form output, though more terse and less warm than Opus 4.8 by default; style is prompt-tunable.

data-analysis.notes: Excellent analytical depth; high-resolution vision enables pixel-level chart and figure transcription.

research-assistant.notes: Strong deep research with 1M context and improved file-based memory; Opus 4.8 improves further on long-horizon coherence.

legal-compliance.notes: Strong privacy posture (SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA-eligible) and literal instruction following suited to compliance pipelines.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA eligible with training opt-out by default; high-resolution vision aids medical document and chart understanding.

financial-analysis.notes: Excellent quantitative reasoning; pixel-accurate chart reading and 1M context handle full filings and figures.

education.notes: Clear, precise explanations with effort-adjustable depth; more literal style benefits structured curricula.

creative-writing.notes: Capable but more clipped and direct than Opus 4.8's warmer voice; no sampling parameters, so variety must be prompted.

strengths: 64.3% SWE-Bench Pro and 94.2% GPQA Diamond at launch
First Claude with high-resolution vision: 2576px long edge with pixel-accurate coordinates
Introduced the xhigh effort level and task budgets (beta) for agentic token control
1M token context window with 128K max output at $5/$25
More literal, predictable instruction following for tuned pipelines
Strong compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA-eligible, training opt-out by default
limitations: Superseded by Opus 4.8 as the current flagship Opus (same price, drop-in upgrade)
Adaptive thinking only — budget_tokens and temperature/top_p/top_k return 400
Thinking content omitted by default; summarized display requires opt-in
Full-resolution images can use up to ~3x more image tokens than prior models
Reaches for tools and subagents less often than Opus 4.6 without explicit prompting
best_for: Vision-heavy workloads: screenshots, computer use, chart and document understanding
Structured extraction and tuned pipelines that benefit from literal instruction following
Long-horizon agentic coding where Opus 4.8 has not yet been qualified
Teams pinned to a validated model version for reproducibility
not_recommended_for: New deployments where Opus 4.8 is available at the same price with better performance
Cost-sensitive high-volume inference (use Sonnet or Haiku tiers)
Workflows that depend on temperature/top_p sampling controls

pricing.notes: 1M context at standard API pricing with no long-context premium. Batch API 50% discount, prompt caching savings apply. No fast-mode variant.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with Constitutional AI alignment; adaptive thinking only with effort parameter introducing xhigh; high-resolution vision
